explicit_text: I gotta tell you about my cousin, Yallah Hammuh. He was one bad guy, so bad that even the sheriff was scared to go near his house at night. Yallah Hammuh lived in a mill town where the canal was drained every week to see if anyone had been bumped off and thrown in. Most weeks, it seemed like Yallah Hammuh was involved in some trouble, but the sheriff never really did much to him until Pick-Ankle Slim stepped in. Despite all that, Yallah Hammuh was the baddest man in town and my cousin, so I know him well.

Most weeks, it seemed like he was involved in some trouble, but the sheriff never really did much to him until another notorious figure stepped in. Despite all that, he was the baddest man in town and my cousin, so I know him well.

explicit_text: Pick-Ankle Slim was an even badder man than Yallah Hammuh, and that’s saying something because Yallah was pretty bad. He showed up one night at a shindig where Yallah was dancing with a nice gal, and things got tense real quick. Slim was tall and skinny with slow eyes that seemed to see right through you, and when he pulled out his gun and shot out the lights, chaos broke loose. Later, Yallah found out that Pick-Ankle Slim was the one who finally gave the sheriff a reason to act, and every time they drained the canal, folks thought Slim might be the one responsible for the trouble. He was a real force in town, and everyone knew it.

explicit_text: When I think about bad men, Stagolee comes to mind as one of the baddest, right up there with Pick-Ankle Slim. Folks around here would say Pick-Ankle Slim was as bad as Stagolee, which tells you just how feared these men were. While I didn’t have personal run-ins with Stagolee, his reputation was well known and often mentioned when talking about the toughest and meanest men around. Comparing Pick-Ankle Slim to Stagolee was a way to show just how dangerous Slim really was in our town.
